report_name: U.S. Governments Brace for Sophisticated AI-Driven Cyber Threats

threat_actors.name: Chinese state-sponsored group
threat_actors.description: A suspected Chinese state-sponsored group that reportedly used an AI tool for a large-scale attack in a 2025 incident, cited as the first documented case of AI-driven threats becoming a reality.

threat_actors.name: Hostile nation-states
threat_actors.description: Hostile nation-states are identified as a primary threat, expected to weaponize frontier AI models to automate hacking on a massive scale against U.S. governments and critical infrastructure.

threat_actors.name: Criminal groups
threat_actors.description: Criminal groups are identified as a significant threat, expected to weaponize frontier AI models to automate hacking on a massive scale.

attack_patterns.name: Active Scanning
attack_patterns.description: The article states that a primary threat of AI is its ability to perform automated reconnaissance by scanning vast networks and codebases for weaknesses at an unprecedented scale.

attack_patterns.name: Develop Capabilities
attack_patterns.description: The article identifies the capability of AI to automatically write functional exploit code for discovered vulnerabilities as a key component of the emerging threat landscape.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The report highlights the fear that agentic AI systems could autonomously exploit zero-day vulnerabilities in public-facing applications, leading to widespread, unexpected intrusions.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Social Engineering LLM
attack_patterns.description: The report describes the threat of AI being used to generate highly personalized and convincing phishing emails and vishing scripts at scale for social engineering attacks.

tools.name: GPT-5.5-Cyber
tools.description: A specialized AI model offered by OpenAI to help defenders find and fix vulnerabilities in their own systems, representing a strategy of using AI to fight AI.

vulnerabilities.name: Zero-Day Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: A class of unknown software vulnerabilities that adversaries, potentially using AI, could discover and exploit at an unprecedented scale.

identities.name: U.S. State and Local Governments
identities.description: State and local governments across the United States identified as being on high alert and preparing for AI-driven cyberattacks.

identities.name: OpenAI
identities.description: An AI company collaborating with critical infrastructure entities and offering its specialized model, GPT-5.5-Cyber, to help defenders find security flaws.

identities.name: Donald Trump
identities.description: U.S. President who signed a recent executive order on AI and security to address the threat of AI-driven attacks.

identities.name: Alan Fuller
identities.description: CIO of Utah who expressed concern about the democratization of advanced hacking capabilities through powerful AI models.
